teamA.name: Duke
teamA.whyWin: A classic safe pick with championship expectations.
teamA.heartFact: Duke has instant name recognition, even for casual fans.
teamA.funFact: The Boozer twins are one of the tournament’s biggest legacy storylines.

teamB.name: Siena
teamB.whyWin: They would need one of the biggest upsets in tournament history.
teamB.heartFact: 16 seeds are the ultimate “what if?” pick.
teamB.funFact: Siena would love to shock the bracket world in Duke’s opening game.

teamA.name: Ohio State
teamA.whyWin: This is close enough that a steadier favorite can feel safer.
teamA.heartFact: A familiar program that casual fans recognize instantly.
teamA.funFact: 8-vs-9 games are usually among the hardest to call.

teamB.name: TCU
teamB.whyWin: A very realistic upset in what is basically an even game.
teamB.heartFact: 9 seeds are great for people who want a little boldness without going wild.
teamB.funFact: The winner gets a brutal second-round draw with Duke looming.

teamA.name: St. John's
teamA.whyWin: A safer pick with real recent form.
teamA.heartFact: Rick Pitino has St. John’s feeling big and fun again.
teamA.funFact: St. John’s repeated as Big East tournament champion coming into the field.

teamB.name: Northern Iowa
teamB.whyWin: 12-over-5 is always tempting.
teamB.heartFact: This is exactly the type of underdog people adopt fast.
teamB.funFact: Northern Iowa won Arch Madness to punch its ticket.

teamA.name: Kansas
teamA.whyWin: Many brackets will trust Kansas almost automatically.
teamA.heartFact: Kansas is one of those names that feels safe to non-sports people.
teamA.funFact: Brand-name 4 seeds usually get picked heavily in office pools.

teamB.name: Cal Baptist
teamB.whyWin: Could become a fan-favorite upset pick fast.
teamB.heartFact: They have great “who are they? now I’m rooting for them” energy.
teamB.funFact: Cal Baptist is one of the first-time participants in the 2026 field.

teamA.name: Louisville
teamA.whyWin: A fairly stable first-round pick on paper.
teamA.heartFact: This is a recognizable name that casual fans may trust quickly.
teamA.funFact: 6-vs-11 games are danger spots for favorites every year.

teamB.name: South Florida
teamB.whyWin: 11 seeds are often the “smart upset” pick.
teamB.heartFact: They bring genuine Cinderella energy into this matchup.
teamB.funFact: South Florida won the American tournament to get here.

teamA.name: Michigan State
teamA.whyWin: A very comfortable higher-seed pick.
teamA.heartFact: This is the kind of program people trust in March almost by habit.
teamA.funFact: 3 seeds are usually expected to survive the first round.

teamB.name: North Dakota State
teamB.whyWin: Would need a hot shooting night and real chaos.
teamB.heartFact: They’re easy to root for if you love an underdog story.
teamB.funFact: 14-over-3 is rare, but it absolutely happens.

teamA.name: UCLA
teamA.whyWin: The bigger brand will make this a common casual pick.
teamA.heartFact: UCLA is one of the easiest schools for non-sports fans to recognize.
teamA.funFact: UCF is coached by Johnny Dawkins, adding a fun Duke connection to the region.

teamB.name: UCF
teamB.whyWin: A realistic upset without being a wild one.
teamB.heartFact: A 10 seed is great for players who want just a little spice.
teamB.funFact: 7-vs-10 is one of the most toss-up-feeling matchups in the bracket.

teamA.name: UConn
teamA.whyWin: A very safe first-round pick with championship-level credibility.
teamA.heartFact: UConn still feels like a March giant.
teamA.funFact: UConn won national titles in 2023 and 2024 and is back as a 2 seed.

teamB.name: Furman
teamB.whyWin: Would need one of the biggest opening-round shocks.
teamB.heartFact: 15 seeds always get a little sentimental love.
teamB.funFact: Furman got here by winning the Southern Conference tournament.

teamA.name: Arizona
teamA.whyWin: One of the safest opening picks on the board.
teamA.heartFact: A 1 seed casual fans can trust quickly.
teamA.funFact: Arizona comes in after winning both the Big 12 regular season and tournament titles.

teamB.name: LIU
teamB.whyWin: Would need a true all-time upset.
teamB.heartFact: 16 seeds are the ultimate chaos pick.
teamB.funFact: LIU is coached by former NBA standout Rod Strickland.

teamA.name: Villanova
teamA.whyWin: A lot of people will lean on the program name here.
teamA.heartFact: Villanova still feels familiar and trustworthy to casual fans.
teamA.funFact: 8-vs-9 is basically bracket coin-flip territory.

teamB.name: Utah State
teamB.whyWin: A very reasonable slightly-upset pick.
teamB.heartFact: Perfect for people who want to be a little bold but not reckless.
teamB.funFact: Utah State won the Mountain West tournament to get here.

teamA.name: Wisconsin
teamA.whyWin: The safer line if you do not want to chase a 12 seed.
teamA.heartFact: Feels dependable for cautious bracket players.
teamA.funFact: 5-vs-12 is the classic upset seed combo every year.

teamB.name: High Point
teamB.whyWin: Exactly the kind of team people pick for chaos.
teamB.heartFact: Very adoptable underdog energy.
teamB.funFact: High Point is one of the first-time participants in the 2026 field.

teamA.name: Arkansas
teamA.whyWin: A stable higher-seed pick.
teamA.heartFact: A recognizable SEC name for casual players.
teamA.funFact: Arkansas won the SEC tournament heading into March Madness.

teamB.name: Hawai'i
teamB.whyWin: Could instantly become a fan-favorite upset team.
teamB.heartFact: Hawai'i stands out in a bracket immediately.
teamB.funFact: Some teams just win on vibes alone, and Hawai'i is one of them.

teamA.name: BYU
teamA.whyWin: A more stable first-round pick on paper.
teamA.heartFact: A comfortable choice if you want something between bold and safe.
teamA.funFact: This spot is waiting on a First Four winner.

teamB.name: TBD
teamB.whyWin: 11 seeds are scary enough before you add play-in momentum.
teamB.heartFact: Play-in winners are easy for people to rally around.
teamB.funFact: This team comes from the Texas vs. NC State First Four matchup.

teamA.name: Gonzaga
teamA.whyWin: A very comfortable pick for many brackets.
teamA.heartFact: Gonzaga has become a true March staple.
teamA.funFact: Gonzaga won the WCC tournament and landed as a 3 seed in the West.

teamB.name: Kennesaw State
teamB.whyWin: Would need a near-perfect game and some March magic.
teamB.heartFact: Small-school upsets are what casual fans remember most.
teamB.funFact: Kennesaw State won Conference USA as a 6 seed.

teamA.name: Miami (Fla.)
teamA.whyWin: Slightly safer if you want the higher-seed route.
teamA.heartFact: The Miami name pops for a lot of casual users.
teamA.funFact: Miami is now coached by Jai Lucas.

teamB.name: Missouri
teamB.whyWin: This is a classic 10-over-7 temptation.
teamB.heartFact: Great choice if you want to be a little bold without going wild.
teamB.funFact: Missouri’s coach Mike Mitchell is a former Duke star, adding another fun connection.

teamA.name: Purdue
teamA.whyWin: One of the safer picks in the opening round.
teamA.heartFact: 2 seeds are comfort picks for bracket makers.
teamA.funFact: Purdue reached this spot after winning the Big Ten tournament.

teamB.name: Queens
teamB.whyWin: Would need one of the biggest surprises of the round.
teamB.heartFact: They have instant underdog appeal.
teamB.funFact: Queens is one of the first-time participants in the 2026 field.

teamA.name: Florida
teamA.whyWin: Florida is one of the strongest and safest opening picks.
teamA.heartFact: Defending champs naturally pull confidence from casual users.
teamA.funFact: Florida is the No. 1 seed in the South Region.

teamB.name: TBD
teamB.whyWin: Would need a historic upset.
teamB.heartFact: Play-in winners are easy to root for.
teamB.funFact: This slot will be filled after the First Four.

teamA.name: Clemson
teamA.whyWin: A reasonable pick if you want the slightly more familiar side.
teamA.heartFact: Feels like a recognizable but not overpowering choice.
teamA.funFact: Iowa is actually the 9 seed in this South Region matchup.

teamB.name: Iowa
teamB.whyWin: This is barely an upset if you choose the 9 seed.
teamB.heartFact: Perfect for someone who wants to zag just a little.
teamB.funFact: This is one of those games many pools will split almost evenly.

teamA.name: Vanderbilt
teamA.whyWin: The safer path unless you want the classic 12-over-5 gamble.
teamA.heartFact: A fun pick for someone who wants a good team that is not too obvious.
teamA.funFact: Vanderbilt gets McNeese in the famous 5-vs-12 trap line.

teamB.name: McNeese
teamB.whyWin: This is one of the first upset lines many people circle.
teamB.heartFact: Exactly the type of team casual fans fall in love with.
teamB.funFact: McNeese is one of the mid-major champions in this year’s field.

teamA.name: Nebraska
teamA.whyWin: A cleaner higher-seed choice on paper.
teamA.heartFact: A good pick if you want strong-seed comfort without going full chalk.
teamA.funFact: Nebraska’s second-round path could include Vanderbilt.

teamB.name: Troy
teamB.whyWin: A true bracket spoiler candidate if Nebraska slips.
teamB.heartFact: 13 seeds are fun because they feel bold but still believable.
teamB.funFact: South bracket previews have already flagged Troy as one of the region’s sleepers.

teamA.name: North Carolina
teamA.whyWin: The name alone will pull a lot of brackets.
teamA.heartFact: Casual fans trust UNC almost automatically.
teamA.funFact: Big-name schools often get picked over pure seed logic.

teamB.name: VCU
teamB.whyWin: 11-over-6 is one of the classic smart-upset choices.
teamB.heartFact: A really appealing pick if you want a Cinderella feel with real logic.
teamB.funFact: VCU won the A-10 tournament heading into the field.

teamA.name: Illinois
teamA.whyWin: A solid higher-seed choice if you want fewer early surprises.
teamA.heartFact: A comfortable pick for people who do not want bracket chaos yet.
teamA.funFact: Illinois landed as the 3 seed in Florida’s South Region.

teamB.name: Penn
teamB.whyWin: Would need a near-perfect game and an Illinois wobble.
teamB.heartFact: People love a smart-school upset angle.
teamB.funFact: Penn reached the field after winning the Ivy League title.

teamA.name: Saint Mary's
teamA.whyWin: A slightly safer route in a very real toss-up.
teamA.heartFact: A team bracket regulars often respect.
teamA.funFact: Saint Mary’s is making its fifth consecutive NCAA appearance.

teamB.name: Texas A&M
teamB.whyWin: A very reasonable first-round upset pick.
teamB.heartFact: 10 seeds feel bold without feeling reckless.
teamB.funFact: This is another strong 7-vs-10 “smart upset” matchup.

teamA.name: Houston
teamA.whyWin: One of the safest opening choices in the bracket.
teamA.heartFact: A great pick if you want strong defense and stability.
teamA.funFact: Houston opens against Big Sky champ Idaho in the South.

teamB.name: Idaho
teamB.whyWin: Would need one of the biggest shocks of the round.
teamB.heartFact: 15 seeds always get sympathy and curiosity.
teamB.funFact: Idaho reached the field by winning the Big Sky.

teamA.name: Michigan
teamA.whyWin: One of the strongest opening-round choices in the tournament.
teamA.heartFact: A 1 seed many people will trust immediately.
teamA.funFact: Michigan is the No. 1 seed in the Midwest Region.

teamB.name: TBD
teamB.whyWin: Would need a historic upset.
teamB.heartFact: Play-in winners always get a little extra bracket affection.
teamB.funFact: This slot will be filled after the First Four.

teamA.name: Georgia
teamA.whyWin: A close game where physicality could matter.
teamA.heartFact: An SEC team casual fans may feel comfortable taking.
teamA.funFact: This is another classic 8-vs-9 coin flip.

teamB.name: Saint Louis
teamB.whyWin: A realistic upset that barely counts as one.
teamB.heartFact: Good pick if you want to be a little different.
teamB.funFact: Saint Louis enters with an 11th NCAA tournament appearance.

teamA.name: Texas Tech
teamA.whyWin: The safer path if you don’t want a 12-seed gamble.
teamA.heartFact: A good pick for players who hate early bracket chaos.
teamA.funFact: But yes, it is still a 5-vs-12 game.

teamB.name: Akron
teamB.whyWin: Exactly the kind of team people pick to bust brackets.
teamB.heartFact: 12 seeds are irresistible to underdog lovers.
teamB.funFact: This is one of the most famous upset lines in all of March Madness.

teamA.name: Alabama
teamA.whyWin: A comfortable higher-seed pick.
teamA.heartFact: A familiar name that makes sense to casual fans.
teamA.funFact: Alabama opens in the Midwest against Hofstra.

teamB.name: Hofstra
teamB.whyWin: Could make Alabama uncomfortable if the favorite goes cold.
teamB.heartFact: 13 seeds are fun because they feel daring but not impossible.
teamB.funFact: Hofstra reached the field by winning the CAA tournament.

teamA.name: Tennessee
teamA.whyWin: Still the stronger side on paper.
teamA.heartFact: Feels like a stable pick if you want moderate risk.
teamA.funFact: This opponent will come from the First Four.

teamB.name: TBD
teamB.whyWin: 11 seeds are already scary before adding play-in momentum.
teamB.heartFact: Play-in winners are among the easiest teams to root for.
teamB.funFact: This team comes from the Miami (Ohio) vs. SMU First Four matchup.

teamA.name: Virginia
teamA.whyWin: A solid safer pick in the Midwest.
teamA.heartFact: Good option for people who like steadier basketball.
teamA.funFact: Virginia would next face Tennessee or the play-in winner if seeds hold.

teamB.name: Wright State
teamB.whyWin: Would need a very sharp shooting day and a Virginia wobble.
teamB.heartFact: 14 seeds are fun if you want a deep underdog story.
teamB.funFact: Wright State is one of the mid-major champions in this year’s field.

teamA.name: Kentucky
teamA.whyWin: The name alone will pull a lot of first-round picks.
teamA.heartFact: Kentucky is one of the easiest teams for casual fans to recognize.
teamA.funFact: Kentucky opens against Santa Clara in the Midwest.

teamB.name: Santa Clara
teamB.whyWin: A very reasonable upset if you want one.
teamB.heartFact: 10 seeds are great for players who want a smart zag.
teamB.funFact: Santa Clara is back in the tournament for the first time since 1996.

teamA.name: Iowa State
teamA.whyWin: One of the stronger and safer picks on the board.
teamA.heartFact: A comfortable chalk choice if you want fewer surprises.
teamA.funFact: Iowa State is the 2 seed in the Midwest.

teamB.name: Tennessee State
teamB.whyWin: Would need a major upset script.
teamB.heartFact: 15 seeds always carry some “what if?” charm.
teamB.funFact: This is the kind of upset that would blow up a lot of brackets instantly.
